summary: Start the controlled R1 Authoritative Client Runtime task-graph design after R0_GATE=PASS and R0 closure integration. Create process task files only; do not implement product code.

tests_run.command: gh pr checks 250 --watch; gh pr merge 250 --squash; git fetch origin --prune
tests_run.result: Both required PR checks passed and R0 closure entered origin/main at 7582d0fd1e54fd51683b99214dbff15e8a030a4c.

tests_run.command: git checkout -b codex/rr1-task-graph-001 origin/main; git status --short --branch
tests_run.result: Clean controlled task-graph branch created from the R0 closure main commit.

decisions.decision: Reuse the closed R0 systems_architect for graph design and the closed R0 qa_reviewer for independent review; do not create replacement agents unless resume fails.
The graph task may create RR1 task definitions and coordination evidence only. Product implementation remains frozen until the accepted graph enters origin/main.

risks.summary: An incorrect DAG could expose parallel product tasks before public runtime boundaries are integrated, causing overlapping writers and incompatible protocol decisions.

requested_action: Create the seven roadmap-listed RR1 task JSON files with conservative dependencies, exact ownership and write scopes, explicit runtime and negative-test evidence, and only one smallest foundation task READY after graph integration. Set RR1-TASK-GRAPH-001 to REVIEW, commit the process-only diff, and emit the expected structured systems REVIEW handoff.
